title: Reform in the Gilded Age
description: Practice your skills in this exercise on reform in the Gilded Age.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“You have won no victories worthy the name. You are slaves, every last one of you. . . . Arouse from your slavery, join the Social Democratic Party and vote with us to take possession of the mines of the country and operate them in the interest of the people . . . and then, and only then, will ‘glorious victories’ have been achieved and you and your comrades be free and your families happy.”

>-Source: Eugene V. Debs, 1899

**Debs most likely wrote his account for which of the following reasons?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to promote an alternative vision for US economy
to convince workers to stop striking and boycotting
to persuade workers to quit their jobs at the mines
to convince the government to regulate the mines

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“You have won no victories worthy the name. You are slaves, every last one of you. . . . Arouse from your slavery, join the Social Democratic Party and vote with us to take possession of the mines of the country and operate them in the interest of the people . . . and then, and only then, will ‘glorious victories’ have been achieved and you and your comrades be free and your families happy.”

>-Source: Eugene V. Debs, 1899

**The remarks in the excerpt were most likely given in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: successful strikes against business leaders
dissolution of the labor movement
rise of sharecropping in the South
use of children as a labor force

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“You have won no victories worthy the name. You are slaves, every last one of you. . . . Arouse from your slavery, join the Social Democratic Party and vote with us to take possession of the mines of the country and operate them in the interest of the people . . . and then, and only then, will ‘glorious victories’ have been achieved and you and your comrades be free and your families happy.”

>-Source: Eugene V. Debs, 1899

**Based on the excerpt, Debs was most likely a member of which group?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Socialists
Nativists
Agrarians
Capitalists

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Image showing women dressed as knights wielding axes chopping up barrels labeled "Gin," "Whiskey," and "Rum." They carry banners labeled "In the Name of God and Humanity" and "Temperance League."](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/226135d4541c6b08b2ec60918d5a7e31.jpg)
*Image titled "Woman's Holy War," showing women dressed as knights wielding axes, chopping up barrels labeled gin, whisky, and rum. They carry banners labeled "In the Name of God and Humanity" and "Temperance League."* Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Womans-Holy-War.jpg), 1874.

**The image most directly reflects which of the following developments during the late nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increased participation of women in voluntary social reform organizations
the increasing power and influence of women within major religious groups
the emergence of a temperance movement to end the sale of alcohol
the creation of settlement houses to improve immigrant lives in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Image showing women dressed as knights wielding axes chopping up barrels labeled "Gin," "Whiskey," and "Rum." They carry banners labeled "In the Name of God and Humanity" and "Temperance League."](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/226135d4541c6b08b2ec60918d5a7e31.jpg)
*Image titled "Woman's Holy War," showing women dressed as knights wielding axes, chopping up barrels labeled gin, whisky, and rum. They carry banners labeled "In the Name of God and Humanity" and "Temperance League."* Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Womans-Holy-War.jpg), 1874.

**Which of the following developments most directly contributed to women’s increased participation in voluntary organizations as depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: improvements in technologies that reduced household labor
extension of suffrage to women through the Fifteenth Amendment
emergence of the popular belief in Republican Motherhood
expansion of the manufacturing labor force to include women

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Image showing women dressed as knights wielding axes chopping up barrels labeled "Gin," "Whiskey," and "Rum." They carry banners labeled "In the Name of God and Humanity" and "Temperance League."](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/226135d4541c6b08b2ec60918d5a7e31.jpg)
*Image titled "Woman's Holy War," showing women dressed as knights wielding axes, chopping up barrels labeled gin, whisky, and rum. They carry banners labeled "In the Name of God and Humanity" and "Temperance League."* Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Womans-Holy-War.jpg), 1874.

**Which of the following statements best describes the perspective expressed in the image about women in the late nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They sought to fix social issues and advocated for moral reform.
They aimed to teach their children good republican values.
They pursued the extension of the right to vote to all women.
They wanted more access to higher education to improve their careers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“Five years ago we had no colored women’s clubs outside of those formed for special work; today with little over a month’s notice, we are able to call representatives from more than twenty clubs. 

>“. . . we need to talk over those things that are of special interest to us as colored women, the training of our children, openings for our boys and girls . . . how to make the most of our own, to some extent, limited opportunities. These are some of our own peculiar questions to be discussed. Besides these are the general questions of the day, which we cannot afford to be indifferent to: temperance, morality, the higher education, hygienic and domestic questions.”

>-Source: Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin, keynote for the Women’s Era Club Conference, 1895

**The excerpt most strongly suggests that in 1895 which of the following was correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: White women and black women had separate voluntary organizations.
Black women started and ran all voluntary organizations in the United States.
White women encouraged black women to work in voluntary organizations.
Black women did not seek to fight for social reform through voluntary work.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“Five years ago we had no colored women’s clubs outside of those formed for special work; today with little over a month’s notice, we are able to call representatives from more than twenty clubs. 

>“. . . we need to talk over those things that are of special interest to us as colored women, the training of our children, openings for our boys and girls . . . how to make the most of our own, to some extent, limited opportunities. These are some of our own peculiar questions to be discussed. Besides these are the general questions of the day, which we cannot afford to be indifferent to: temperance, morality, the higher education, hygienic and domestic questions.”

>-Source: Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin, keynote for the Women’s Era Club Conference, 1895

**The key concern that the excerpt was designed to address was the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: lack of equality for black women.
lynching epidemic in southern states.
ongoing wage inequality for black men.
continued segregation in public spaces.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“Five years ago we had no colored women’s clubs outside of those formed for special work; today with little over a month’s notice, we are able to call representatives from more than twenty clubs. 

>“. . . we need to talk over those things that are of special interest to us as colored women, the training of our children, openings for our boys and girls . . . how to make the most of our own, to some extent, limited opportunities. These are some of our own peculiar questions to be discussed. Besides these are the general questions of the day, which we cannot afford to be indifferent to: temperance, morality, the higher education, hygienic and domestic questions.”

>-Source: Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin, keynote for the Women’s Era Club Conference, 1895

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how women organized to promote social and political reform
how black women pushed for desegregation laws in Congress
how women’s roles changed because of industrialization
how women gained financial support for women’s colleges

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the table and answer the question below.

|| **Historical summary of higher education enrollment statistics, 1869 to 1900** ||
 | 1869-1870 | 1879-1880 | 1889-1890 | 1899-1900
- | - | - | - | - 
**Male** | 49,467| 77,994 | 100,453 | 152,254
**Female** | 13,372 | 37,856 | 56,303 | 85,338

Source: Estimates of enrollment provided by the [Department of Education](https://nces.ed.gov/pubs93/93442.pdf), 1993

**Which of the following statements best describes the change over time in higher education enrollment depicted in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: More men and women attended higher education institutions in the 1899-1900 school year than they did in the 1869-1870 school year.
Women’s enrollment in higher education peaked in the 1879-1880 school year, but reduced in subsequent years.
Men’s enrollment in higher education peaked in the 1889-1890 school year, but reduced in the following years.
More men and women attended higher education institutions in the 1889-1890 school year than they did in the 1899-1900 school year.

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the table and answer the question below.

|| **Historical summary of higher education enrollment statistics, 1869 to 1900** ||
 | 1869-1870 | 1879-1880 | 1889-1890 | 1899-1900
- | - | - | - | - 
**Male** | 49,467| 77,994 | 100,453 | 152,254
**Female** | 13,372 | 37,856 | 56,303 | 85,338

Source: Estimates of enrollment provided by the [Department of Education](https://nces.ed.gov/pubs93/93442.pdf), 1993

**The overall trend from 1869 to 1900 depicted in the table was most directly caused by which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increase in the number of co-ed and all-women’s colleges
the replacement of women workers with immigrant workers
the change in women’s roles within their households
the rise of voluntary organizations to advocate for women’s education

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the table and answer the question below.

|| **Historical summary of higher education enrollment statistics, 1869 to 1900** ||
 | 1869-1870 | 1879-1880 | 1889-1890 | 1899-1900
- | - | - | - | - 
**Male** | 49,467| 77,994 | 100,453 | 152,254
**Female** | 13,372 | 37,856 | 56,303 | 85,338

Source: Estimates of enrollment provided by the [Department of Education](https://nces.ed.gov/pubs93/93442.pdf), 1993

**Which of the following was a direct effect of the trend in higher education shown in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: new employment opportunities previously unavailable to women opened up
fewer voluntary organizations operated as women’s colleges discouraged them
fewer women worked in factory jobs as more women entered college instead
more women participated in party politics and voted in federal elections
